steps.title: Identify the required appositive structure
steps.content: After the subject noun “The hypothesized protolanguage from which all Kartvelian languages have descended,” the comma signals that an appositive must follow—a noun phrase that renames or identifies this subject.

steps.title: Match the subject to its renaming appositive
steps.content: Choice B correctly supplies “Proto-Kartvelian” as an appositive that identifies what the hypothesized protolanguage is, followed by the main predicate “has been reconstructed by experts, who inferred the language's linguistic properties from those of its descendants.” The other three choices violate the appositive rule by introducing noun phrases that don't rename the subject—they describe properties of descendants or introduce unrelated agents like “the experts” instead. That matches choice B — B is correct.
